job_title: Business Analyst – HR Data & Metadata Management
strengths: Strong technical skills (SQL, Python, Snowflake, BigQuery)
Proven automation and ETL/ELT pipeline development
Experience with metadata and data validation (Great Expectations)
Quantified impact on reporting accuracy and efficiency
weaknesses: No stated experience with data privacy/regulatory frameworks
RAID/status reporting and formal delivery management not detailed
Limited explicit experience with data access control
No certifications specific to data governance
improvement_bullets: Standardized organizational KPIs and data governance frameworks, implementing data access control protocols and documentation to ensure secure HR-aligned data classification and cross-functional compliance.
Managed metadata management and data cataloging initiatives by creating centralized documentation, ensuring data lineage accuracy and improving discoverability for business stakeholders.
Led project delivery management through proactive RAID reporting and milestone tracking, ensuring cross-functional initiatives were completed under tight timelines in an Agile environment.
Developed data quality monitoring procedures and validation schemas to uphold data privacy standards and regulatory compliance across internal data assets.
Integrated disparate datasets into a structured data catalog, focusing on HR data domains and employee platform metadata to drive organizational trust and reporting transparency.
cover_letter: Hiring Manager, Jobs via Dice, New York, NY. Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Business Analyst – HR Data & Metadata Management opportunity with Jobs via Dice. I am interested in this opportunity because of the critical intersection of data governance and HR strategic operations. What interests me about working for Jobs via Dice is its commitment to facilitating high-quality connections between talent and industry leaders; this mission inspires me because I am passionate about the structural integrity required to manage sensitive workforce information effectively. I believe my expertise in data governance frameworks and my proficiency in metadata management would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems with a focus on standardization and accuracy. In fact, while at Shine Masters, I identified significant inconsistencies in our reporting workflows and led the effort to standardize our KPI frameworks and data governance documentation. This created a 'single source of truth' that reduced reporting discrepancies by 40% and enabled leadership to make data-driven decisions with full confidence in our data lineage. Ultimately, I know that the Business Analyst – HR Data & Metadata Management role at Jobs via Dice would allow me to bring my data classification expertise to your HR data asset inventory and governance projects. Additionally, my role as a Data Analyst & Operations Lead at Shine Masters enabled me to develop my project delivery management skills. I was responsible for tracking project milestones and managing stakeholder expectations, ensuring that complex technical tasks like A/B testing and automation development were delivered within tight timelines. This experience translates directly to the RAID reporting and status tracking required for your HR data initiatives. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my data quality monitoring skills in working on HR data classification and metadata management at Jobs via Dice. I know that my technical background in Python and SQL and my commitment to data governance align with what it takes to succeed at the Business Analyst – HR Data & Metadata Management role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
